channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Lawmakers address red tape holding residents back from rebuilding after storms
item.description: The blame game continues on the local, state, and federal levels as officials discuss who can be held accountable for hurricane relief efforts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DeSantis hopes Trump will kickstart crackdown on illegal immigration
item.description: "There's been no issue that has been higher on the radar," DeSantis added. "We need all hands on deck.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson removes Mike Turner as Intelligence Committee chair
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) will not make Rep. Mike Turner (R-Ohio) chair of the House Intelligence Committee in the 119th Congress, a source familiar with the decision confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to give farewell address from Oval Office as he prepares to cede power to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden will deliver a farewell address to the nation Wednesday night, seizing what may be his final opportunity to reshape Americans' grim views on his term before he departs the White House. He plans to deliver the speech at 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two Americans to be released from Gaza in first phase of ceasefire deal
item.description: Two Americans, Keith Siegel and Sagui Dekel-Chen, are expected to be released as part of the first phase of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal that emerged Wednesday after being held for 15-months by Hamas since the start of the war in Gaza, according to a senior administration official.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who is Trump's attorney general pick Pam Bondi? The former prosecutor is a close Trump ally
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump's pick to lead the Justice Department, Pam Bondi, faced questions on Capitol Hill Wednesday over her loyalty to the Republican president-elect, who has vowed to use the agency to pursue revenge on his perceived political enemies.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: End to daylight saving time among bills for 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: A Republican lawmaker who represents parts of six rural Nevada counties is sponsoring a bill to take the state off daylight saving time — one of the 200 bills written so far.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Marco Rubio warns China is America's 'biggest threat,' affirms value of NATO alliance
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Florida Sen. Marco Rubio on Wednesday painted a dark vision of the consequences of America's “unbalanced relationship” with China, echoing President-elect Donald Trump's anti-globalist rhetoric as he vies to be confirmed as his secretary of state.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, DIDI TANG and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the special election for Vargas' Board of Supervisors seat
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors on Tuesday finalized plans to fill the seat formerly held by Nora Vargas before her abrupt exit from elected office through a special election.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Rubio vows to place US interests 'above all else' as Trump's top diplomat
item.description: Florida Sen. Marco Rubio promised on Wednesday to implement President-elect Donald Trump’s “America First” vision as secretary of state, vowing in his confirmation hearing that the incoming administration will forge a new path by placing American interests “above all else.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATTHEW LEE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden admin plows ahead with 11th hour plan to effectively ban cigarettes
item.description: The Biden administration's Food and Drug Administration (FDA) released additional details on a proposed rule that would effectively ban cigarettes currently on the market.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AG nominee Pam Bondi won't say Trump lost 2020 election
item.description: Attorney general nominee Pam Bondi dodged a question from Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) on Wednesday about whether President-elect Trump lost the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida's Rubio and Bondi face confirmation hearings
item.description: Former state attorney general and Tampa native Pam Bondi and Senator Marco Rubio are set to appear on Capitol Hill for confirmation hearings, Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Few US adults confident Justice Department and FBI will act fairly under Trump, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: As President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general, Pam Bondi, prepares for questioning from senators on Wednesday, only about 2 in 10 U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden promised to turn the page on Trump. Now he's being replaced by him
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Joe Biden promised nothing short of a national exorcism when he took office. He wanted to “restore the soul" of the country and prove that Donald Trump was only a footnote in the American story, not its next chapter.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth confronts allegations of misconduct as senators grill Trump's choice for Pentagon chief
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump's choice for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, vowed Tuesday to foster a “warrior culture” at the Pentagon and confronted allegations of sexual assault and excessive drinking and questions about his derisive views of women in combat during a heated Senat
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, TARA COPP and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden moves to lift state sponsor of terrorism designation for Cuba, part of deal to free prisoners
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden notified Congress of his intent to lift the U.S. designation of Cuba as a state sponsor of terrorism, the White House announced, as part of a deal facilitated by the Catholic Church to free political prisoners on the island. Senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, COLLEEN LONG, MATTHEW LEE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. attorneys for Michigan resign before Trump takes office
item.description: Both of Michigan's U.S. attorneys have announced they will resign from their posts before Donald Trump is sworn into office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: House barrels toward fight over placing conditions on California fire aid
item.description: The House is barreling toward a contentious debate over disaster relief for California as many — but not all — Republicans call for conditions to be placed on the assistance, an idea Democrats are soundly rejecting. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans pass bill to ban transgender athletes from girls’ sports
item.description: House Republicans passed a bill Tuesday to bar transgender student-athletes from competing in girls’ sports, putting the contentious issue front and center following an election cycle that saw it become a frequent target of Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota House DFLers boycott opening session; Republicans elect their own speaker
item.description: DFLers on Tuesday made good on their threat to keep the Minnesota House in limbo as the parties fight over control. House Republicans pushed ahead anyway in the half-empty chamber. It’s all likely headed to the courts.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: David Weiss condemns pardon, defends record in Hunter Biden prosecution report
item.description: Special Counsel David Weiss defended his handling of the Hunter Biden investigation in a final report capping his multi-year investigation into the president’s son.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Judge Cannon paves the way for potential release of part 1 of special counsel’s report
item.description: Cannon denied a motion by President-elect Donald Trump's former co-defendants who sought to block the release of special counsel Jack Smith's report on the election interference case.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Exit interview: DOT Sec. Buttigieg on infrastructure act and the road ahead
item.description: In an exit interview with All Things Considered, DOT Secretary Pete Buttigieg reflects on the Biden administration's infrastructure act and why it didn't resonate with some voters.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow, Avery Keatley, and Adam Raney, MPR News

item.title: People are rushing to install solar panels before Trump becomes President
item.description: Potential rooftop solar customers and installers worry the incoming Trump administration might try to eliminate a 30 percent federal tax credit. Some customers plan to install sooner because of that and solar companies are changing their business plans.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady, MPR News

item.title: Biden awards Pope Francis the Presidential Medal of Freedom with distinction
item.description: The White House commended Pope Francis for his commitment to serving the poor, as well as advocating for peace and protecting the planet.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Biden takes unexpected, impromptu questions from press in final days
item.description: President Biden opted Friday to voluntarily take a host of questions from the press after delivering remarks on the day’s job report, a speech that had been put on the White House schedule last minute.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Pinellas residents struggle with permit delays, seek accountability
item.description: Many Pinellas County residents have been displaced from their homes since Hurricane Helene hit more than 100 days ago. Some have nowhere else to go, forcing them to stay in damaged and unfinished homes until the county gives them the permits to rebuild.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
